oeeDesc: OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) measures how well your equipment is performing. It combines Availability, Performance, and Quality into one percentage. World-class is 85%+.
oeeWhen: Use this to spot where you're losing production time and output.
paretoDesc: Ranks your problems from biggest to smallest. The 80/20 rule: 20% of causes create 80% of problems. Attack the tall bars first.
paretoWhen: Use this after OEE shows losses — find which specific issues matter most.
rcaDesc: Two methods to dig deeper: 5-Why (ask 'why' repeatedly until you find the root cause) and Ishikawa/Fishbone (map all possible causes by category). Don't guess — investigate.
rcaWhen: Use this when you know WHAT the problem is but not WHY it's happening.
kaizenDesc: Every improvement starts here: idea → planned → in progress → completed → verified → standardized. Track savings and follow through to make sure improvements stick.
kaizenWhen: Use this to track every improvement action from idea to completion.
safetyDesc: Log safety incidents, view the Safety Cross calendar, and track days without incidents. Safety is always priority #1 in lean manufacturing.
safetyWhen: Use this to report any safety event and track your factory's safety performance.
sqcdpDesc: The 5 pillars of your shift: Safety, Quality, Cost, Delivery, People. Green/yellow/red at a glance. Use this in your daily tier meeting to review performance.
sqcdpWhen: Use this every day at your team meeting to discuss what needs attention.
andonDesc: Real-time status of every production line. Red = stopped, yellow = warning, green = running. Like a traffic light for your factory. Put this on a big screen on the shop floor.
andonWhen: Use this to see at a glance which lines need help right now.
spcDesc: Statistical Process Control tracks measurements over time. It tells you if variation is normal or if something changed. Red flags mean investigate immediately.
spcWhen: Use this to monitor critical quality measurements and catch drift before it causes defects.
qualityDesc: The quality hub: NCR (Non-Conformance Reports) for documenting defects, CAPA (Corrective/Preventive Actions) for fixing and preventing them, and QC Checks for inspections.
qualityWhen: Use this when a defect is found — document it, investigate it, fix it, prevent it.
gembaDesc: A structured walk through the factory. Observe, ask, listen — don't fix on the spot. Record observations, take photos, assign follow-ups. 'Go to gemba' is the #1 lean principle.
gembaWhen: Use this for regular factory floor walks to see the real situation with your own eyes.
sixSDesc: Walk through your area and score: Sort, Set in order, Shine, Standardize, Sustain (+Safety). Regular audits build discipline and make problems visible.
sixSWhen: Use this weekly or monthly to audit workplace organization in each area.
tpmDesc: Total Productive Maintenance — planned maintenance schedules, autonomous maintenance checklists (CILT), and machine health tracking. Fix before it breaks.
tpmWhen: Use this to schedule preventive maintenance and track machine reliability.
smedDesc: Single-Minute Exchange of Die — systematically reduce the time to switch from Product A to Product B. Separate internal vs external tasks, then streamline.
smedWhen: Use this when changeover times are eating into your production availability.
a3Desc: Structured problem-solving on one page: background, current state, goal, root cause, actions, follow-up. Forces clear thinking. Use for bigger, cross-team problems.
a3When: Use this for complex problems that need cross-functional collaboration and management review.
fmeaDesc: Failure Mode & Effects Analysis — score every potential failure by Severity × Occurrence × Detection. Focus on the highest Risk Priority Numbers first. Proactive, not reactive.
fmeaWhen: Use this proactively to identify and prevent problems before they happen.
pokaYokeDesc: Registry of error-proofing devices and methods. A poka-yoke makes it impossible to do something wrong — like a USB plug that only fits one way.
pokaYokeWhen: Use this to document and track mistake-proofing solutions across your factory.
productionDesc: Enter hourly production counts, downtime minutes, and cycle times. This data feeds directly into your OEE calculations. Accurate input = accurate insights.
productionWhen: Use this every hour or every shift to record what was actually produced.
handoverDesc: Write what happened on your shift — problems, pending work, safety notes. The next shift reads this first. Good handovers prevent repeated mistakes.
handoverWhen: Use this at the end of every shift before you leave.
ordersDesc: Plan what to produce, how much, and when. Orders flow into Production Tracking where operators record actual output. Auto-completes when target is reached.
ordersWhen: Create orders before each production run. Release them when materials and lines are ready.
productsDesc: Define your product catalog with SKUs, cycle times, and Bills of Materials. BOM data auto-populates ideal cycle times in Production Tracking and OEE calculations.
productsWhen: Set up products before creating production orders. Update cycle times when processes change.
